title: Why Google’s AI can’t spell Google (or anything else)
description: Google is embarrassing itself, again.

title: In more good news for Amazon, Snowflake signs $6B deal with AWS for AI CPU chips
description: Snowflake has signed a new, enormous five-year deal with Amazon to secure chips for AI usage. Nvidia is once again being put on notice.

title: Payroll startup Remote says it grew revenue 50% per employee without adding headcount
description: Payroll service provider Remote recently surpassed $300 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR) and became cash-flow positive, thanks to a 50% increase in revenue per employee resulting from AI adoption.

title: Your SEO strategy is optimized for a search engine that no longer exists.
description: Google I/O made it official: AI-generated answers are now front and center in search, and most brands have almost no visibility into how AI is describing them to their customers. For anyone who has spent years building a strategy around 10 blue links, the rules just changed in a pretty significant way. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Rebecca […]

title: Meta launches Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp subscriptions, with more to come, including AI plans
description: Meta is rolling out paid subscription plans for Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp worldwide, while also testing new AI, creator, and business-focused offerings under its broader “Meta One” subscription brand.

title: AI coding startup Cognition raises $1B at $25B pre-money valuation
description: As Cognition reaches $492 million in annualized revenue run rate, it more than doubled its valuation in eight months, it says.

title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
description: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding, gain global visibility, connect directly with investors, and launch on the TechCrunch Disrupt stage.

title: ElevenLabs’ new music-generation model can switch genres mid-track
description: ElevenLabs' new model will let users regenerate a section of a song without affecting the rest of the track.

title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
description: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If you plan to attend one of the most influential gatherings in tech this year, now is the time to lock in your pass before rates go up again.

title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
description: SOND introduced its debut product: Dreambuds, a closed-loop, in-ear system that captures 12 physiological signals from the wearer, then acts on them in real time to help consumers get better sleep.

title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
description: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

title: ClickHouse triples annualized revenue to $250M, charting a path toward an IPO
description: The database provider is eyeing a public debut within the next few years.

title: YouTube will now automatically label AI videos
description: YouTube will now automatically label videos that use significant photorealistic AI, instead of relying solely on creators to disclose AI-generated content themselves. It's also making AI labels more prominent.

title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
description: While these agents would be able to read and analyze users' portfolios to come up with trading strategies and suggest investments, they'll only be able to access the pre-loaded balance in the dedicated wallet to place orders.

title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
description: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.

title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
description: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
description: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
description: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect the real-world physical training data that AI and robotics labs are racing to acquire.

title: Universal Music Group and TikTok renew agreement to combat unauthorized AI music
description: For years, UMG has pushed platforms, streaming services, and AI companies to implement stricter content moderation policies.

title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket rates end May 29
description: Save up to $410 on your TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 pass before prices increase on May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to join the tech epicenter in San Francisco.
